entries.title: *Furni Novi Philosophici* — front cover (binding) (Plate 0)
entries.creator: Johann Rudolph Glauber
entries.repository: [PLACEHOLDER: per source — Internet Archive / BIU Santé scan (res011275x01) of the Latin *Furni Novi Philosophici*]
entries.summary:
A bibliographic leaf — the **front cover (binding)** — of the surviving copy of Glauber's *Furni Novi Philosophici* digitised from the BIU Santé exemplar. This is the physical book, not one of its furnace plates.

## Iconography
Worn leather or vellum over boards, stained and rubbed by handling. No engraved apparatus is present.

## Significance
Recorded honestly as the binding of the volume. The *Furni Novi Philosophici* ("The New Philosophical Furnaces"), first issued at Amsterdam from 1648, is Glauber's foundational treatise on distillation apparatus; this copy carries the Latin text through to the open archive.

## For artists & game designers
A battered leather book cover — a serviceable closed-treatise or grimoire prop.

citations.text: Johann Rudolph Glauber, *Furni Novi Philosophici, sive Descriptio artis distillatoriae novae* (Amsterdam, 1648–1650; this copy a Latin *Opera* text printing, BIU Santé res011275x01).
Kathleen Ahonen, "Johann Rudolph Glauber: A Study of Animism in Seventeenth-Century Chemistry," (diss.).
Internet Archive

entries.title: *Furni Novi Philosophici* — text leaf (Latin) (Plate 8)
entries.creator: Johann Rudolph Glauber
entries.repository: [PLACEHOLDER: per source — Internet Archive / BIU Santé scan (res011275x01) of the Latin *Furni Novi Philosophici*]
entries.summary:
A **letterpress text leaf** from Glauber's *Furni Novi Philosophici*, the Latin treatise on the new philosophical furnaces and the distiller's art. Dense Latin prose under the running head *Furnor. Philosophic.*; no furnace engraving falls on this page.

## Iconography
Roman type in a single justified column, the occasional italic for emphasis or for the names of preparations (*aqua fortis*, *spiritus nitri*). No technical illustration is present on this leaf.

## Significance
Recorded honestly as a text page rather than as one of the work's celebrated furnace plates — the every-eighth-page sampling of this copy fell on the discursive text, not the engraved apparatus. The surrounding matter sets out Glauber's distillation operations: the separation of metals, the making of strong waters, the spirits of nitre and vitriol, the chymical medicines drawn from them.

## For artists & game designers
Usable as an authentic early-modern printed page or letterpress texture.

citations.text: Johann Rudolph Glauber, *Furni Novi Philosophici, sive Descriptio artis distillatoriae novae* (Amsterdam, 1648–1650; this copy a Latin *Opera* text printing, BIU Santé res011275x01).
Kathleen Ahonen, "Johann Rudolph Glauber: A Study of Animism in Seventeenth-Century Chemistry," (diss.).
Internet Archive

entries.title: *Furni Novi Philosophici* — blank / near-blank leaf (Plate 72)
entries.creator: Johann Rudolph Glauber
entries.repository: [PLACEHOLDER: per source — Internet Archive / BIU Santé scan (res011275x01) of the Latin *Furni Novi Philosophici*]
entries.summary:
A blank or near-blank leaf captured in the scan of Glauber's *Furni Novi Philosophici* — a flyleaf or interleaf bearing no print or apparatus.

## Iconography
Untoned paper with show-through and minor foxing. No image content.

## Significance
Recorded honestly as a blank leaf rather than as a furnace engraving. Such pages punctuate the gathering structure of the volume.

## For artists & game designers
Usable only as a plain aged-paper texture.

citations.text: Johann Rudolph Glauber, *Furni Novi Philosophici, sive Descriptio artis distillatoriae novae* (Amsterdam, 1648–1650; this copy a Latin *Opera* text printing, BIU Santé res011275x01).

entries.title: *Furni Novi Philosophici* — Index (*Index rerum*) (Plate 144)
entries.creator: Johann Rudolph Glauber
entries.repository: [PLACEHOLDER: per source — Internet Archive / BIU Santé scan (res011275x01) of the Latin *Furni Novi Philosophici*]
entries.summary:
An **index leaf** from Glauber's *Furni Novi Philosophici* — a column of headwords with page references (here the printed *Index* of contents).

## Iconography
Letterpress set in two columns of entries and folio numbers. No technical illustration is present.

## Significance
Recorded honestly as the volume's index rather than as a furnace plate. The index is nonetheless a guide to Glauber's programme — running through his furnaces, spirits, salts and metallic preparations.

## For artists & game designers
Usable as an authentic early-modern index or reference page.

citations.text: Johann Rudolph Glauber, *Furni Novi Philosophici, sive Descriptio artis distillatoriae novae* (Amsterdam, 1648–1650; this copy a Latin *Opera* text printing, BIU Santé res011275x01).
Kathleen Ahonen, "Johann Rudolph Glauber: A Study of Animism in Seventeenth-Century Chemistry," (diss.).
Internet Archive

The surrounding matter sets out Glauber's distillation operations: the separation of metals, the making of strong waters, the spirits of nitre and vitriol, the chymical medicines drawn from them.

## For artists & game designers
Usable as an authentic early-modern printed page or letterpress texture.

Kathleen Ahonen, "Johann Rudolph Glauber: A Study of Animism in Seventeenth-Century Chemistry," (diss.).
